title: Missing canary runs in CloudWatch console due to timeout without metric emission
symptoms: Canary runs appear missing in CloudWatch console availability graph
Data points missing from console graph despite canary being scheduled
Canary configured to run every X minutes but shows missing runs
Console graph shows incomplete data visualization
root_cause: Runtime versions prior to syn-nodejs-puppeteer-11.0 only emit CloudWatch metrics (SuccessPercent, Duration, etc.) after the canary script completes successfully or fails gracefully. When Lambda times out mid-execution, the runtime never reaches the metric-emission code path, so no metric data point is published for that run. The Synthetics console availability graph is driven by these metrics, so timed-out runs appear as gaps rather than failures. The canary did execute — it just timed out before emitting metrics. This was fixed in syn-nodejs-puppeteer-11.0, which emits metrics even on timeout.

recommendations.title: Upgrade to syn-nodejs-puppeteer-11.0 or later
recommendations.problem: Older runtime versions don't emit metrics when canary times out

solution.description: Identify which runtime version the canary is using
solution.command:
# Via AWS Console:
# 1. Navigate to CloudWatch Synthetics console
# 2. Select your canary
# 3. Check "Configuration" tab for Runtime version

# Via AWS CLI:
aws synthetics get-canary \
  --name <canary-name> \
  --region <canary-region>

solution.description: Upgrade runtime to version that emits metrics on timeout
solution.command:
# Via AWS Console:
# 1. Navigate to CloudWatch Synthetics console
# 2. Select your canary
# 3. Click "Action" and select "Edit" in the dropdown
# 4. Update runtime version from "Runtime version" dropdown to syn-nodejs-puppeteer-11.0 or later
# 5. Save changes

# Via AWS CLI:
aws synthetics update-canary \
  --name <canary-name> \
  --region <canary-region> \
  --runtime-version syn-nodejs-puppeteer-11.0
solution.expected_outcome: Canary updated to new runtime version

solution.description: Confirm that timed-out runs now show in console graph
solution.command:
# Monitor canary runs and check console graph
# Even timed-out runs should now appear as data points
solution.expected_outcome: All canary runs visible in console graph, including timeouts

recommendations.title: Investigate and resolve timeout root cause
recommendations.problem: Canary timing out indicates underlying performance issue

solution.step: Review canary logs for timeout patterns
solution.command:
# Via AWS Console:
# 1. Navigate to CloudWatch Synthetics console
# 2. Select your canary
# 3. Select a failed canary run from the canary run availability graph
# 4. Check the canary run execution log under "Logs" tab. 

# Look for patterns like:
# - Slow page loads
# - Network timeouts
# - Resource exhaustion
solution.expected_outcome: Identified specific timeout cause (e.g., page load > 60s)

solution.description: Adjust canary timeout to accommodate slow operations
solution.expected_outcome: Canary timeout increased, fewer timeout failures

solution.description: Reduce execution time to prevent timeouts
solution.expected_outcome: Canary execution time reduced, fewer timeouts
